name: Stir Fried Spareribs with Green Peppers
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Rack pork ribs, cut lengthwise across bones into halves (bite size)
2 Green onions, with tops, chopped into 2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat and remove membranes from ribs. Cut each rib into bite-sized pieces.
2. In a glass or plastic bowl, toss the ribs with 2 teaspoons cornstarch, soy sauce (light or dark), salt, and 1 teaspoon sugar. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
3. Cut green peppers into 1-inch pieces. Cut green onions into 2-inch pieces.
4. Heat a wok until hot; test by sprinkling 1-2 drops of water in the wok. If they bubble and skitter, it's ready. Add vegetable oil to coat the sides of the wok.
5. Add ribs to the hot wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
6. Add garlic and ginger root, stirring for an additional minute.
7. Stir in brown bean sauce and cook until it just starts to thicken (about 1 minute).
8. Pour in chicken broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
9. In a small bowl, mix cold water with 2 tablespoons cornstarch and 1 teaspoon sugar to form a slurry. Stir the slurry into the ribs and continue cooking until thickened.
10. Add green peppers and cook for another minute.
11. Garnish with chopped green onions before serving.
Do-Ahead Directions: After simmering ribs for 20 minutes, cover and refrigerate no more than 24 hours. Just before serving, heat the ribs to boiling in the wok, cover, and cook for an additional 2 minutes before proceeding with the recipe.
